title: CT2.terminal.ct3.context completed discharge record — CT3 terminal — context residual
description: Concrete completed-discharge schema for semantic CT2-v2 node CT2.terminal.ct3.context. It fixes the runtime identity, presentation order, successful status, and exact contract evidence without converting the semantic ID back to a numbered obligation ID.

tacticId.description: Owning tactic discriminator for CT2.terminal.ct3.context; it must be CT2.

nodeId.description: Stable semantic runtime identifier for CT3 terminal — context residual.

nodeNumber.description: One-based presentation-order index for CT2.terminal.ct3.context; it is not part of the semantic identity.

status.description: Required successful completion state for CT2.terminal.ct3.context, matching its terminal runtime role.

dischargedContracts.description: Exact contract families discharged at CT2.terminal.ct3.context: S-Equiv, S-Rout, S-Trig.

contains.description: Requires S-Equiv, which certifies the selected replacement candidate or records a separating context.
Requires S-Rout, which constructs the declared typed CT3 or CT10 payload.
Requires S-Trig, which certifies that the selected consumer accepts the routed payload.

contractInstances.description: Typed contract instances for CT2.terminal.ct3.context; one S-Equiv, S-Rout, S-Trig record is required for each declared family.

schema.description: Discriminator for the S-Equiv evidence at CT2.terminal.ct3.context; this instance certifies the selected replacement candidate or records a separating context.
Discriminator for the S-Rout evidence at CT2.terminal.ct3.context; this instance constructs the declared typed CT3 or CT10 payload.
Discriminator for the S-Trig evidence at CT2.terminal.ct3.context; this instance certifies that the selected consumer accepts the routed payload.

producedPayloads.description: Payload family delivered by CT2.terminal.ct3.context to CT3.

x-obligation.dischargeCriteria: Supply the declared proof-carrying state, decision, certificate, or payload. Every outgoing edge is available only through its named typed constructor and evidence slots.
